feedback.text: As an avid senior computer user and gamer there's currently a practice that I find worrisome. It is the fact that more and more software (in particular games) are not owned by me, the consumer, but are licensed to me only. An implication of this model is that the provider can end this licensing one-sided and doesn't even have to ensure that the programs and games I paid for are still working (i.e. no consumer friendly end-of-life plan). I interpret this as a very skewed balance of power and very much like to see a better protection of my consumer rights.
